The Aladdin (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 4
Speaker 1: Welcome back, guys. This is Themeplate 10, nicknamed "The Aladdin", and this is what
you're gonna be using as your minimum viable funnel version of your launch e-mail. So
this is if you don't have a webinar, you don't have a sales video, you don't have a sales
page, you don't have time or budget or whatever to create any of those, and you want
to really just link your email to a buy button.
You'll need to spice up this version of the anti-launch e-mail with just those few extra
ingredients that allow it to stand alone without a sales pitch. If you're gonna roll with
this one, I recommend also checking out modules six and seven as we borrow elements
from the sales and closing subsequence to really make this one tick, but the main thing
we're using here is what I call The Magic Carpet Ride method to give your prospect that
vivid walkthrough of your program's main features and benefits in an evocative way that
really allows them to project themselves forward to future [inaudible 00:01:03], and see
themselves participating in those outcomes that your modules and lessons and features
and benefits deliver.
The point is not to make this e-mail about consuming information about your offer. We
do need to communicate that information, but we don't want it to be about consuming
it, we want them to consume the vision of what life will look like using your program,
which accomplishes both. It turns that information and alchemizes it into a consumable
vision.
All that said, here is the themeplate. You will open up with the offer now live moment.
You've had your pre-launch, so it is appropriate to open with that link. Don't call it a
launch. High level outcomes they will achieve. Then you'll dive into that Rome is Burning
moment like we spoke about before, which could include that moment of highest
tension. Why it's not a launch for you, so this is where you add that credibility, proof,
testimonials, case studies, and then unlike the anti-launch launch here you will do a
program walkthrough and stack the value before getting into your offer, which would
also include any surface level urgency or scarcity you're using.
If you are closing your cart later in two days, in three days, in five days, whatever it is,
this is where you would mention it. Then you would challenge your prospect to achieve
that quick, valuable win. And you will position against the alternative, the cost of going
backwards. A few extra little tips that you're gonna be using and integrating in this
version verus the other one. That's what's gonna allow us to do it without a sales pitch.
That is what we are rolling with and then you're gonna close with your minimum viable
commitment, which you'll also learn more about in module seven. This is a speculative
one, this is an imagined draft. This email was never actually sent out, but this is what I
would be doing if the course you're consuming right now didn't have a sales pitch, we
weren't doing webinars, and workshops and all these things to invite you to participate
in, this is what it might look like.
So, "10X launches is now live but please, please, please for the love of all that is good
and furry don't call it a launch 'cause here's the thing. Even though you now have the
ability to" ... and here are those high level outcomes. "Solve your funnel execution crisis
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

The Aladdin (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 4
for good without having your profits held hostage by a top copywriter's availability or
your own 'a little Xanax in my latte won't kill me' anxiety around a blinking cursor ...
Clear away the biggest bottleneck between you and those game changing five and six
figure product launches and funnels that fill your entrepreneurial heart with envy. And
do it all in a way that ditches the needless complexity around crafting high-converting
launch copy and instead leverage and optimize your existing shkills as a coach, educator,
trainer, or expert. Nothing has actually 'launched'."
Here's that Rome is Burning, those specific moments that you really ramp up the heat of
that flame. "You've already been at it. Creating amazing courses and plunking down on
every program and software to hit the market thinking 'this will be the one' that leads
me to my big breakthrough. Quickly realizing that all those funnels and systems you're
buying into, as cool as they are, are all held together by this special connective tissues
thing we call copy. And doing everything in your power to make it work. Waking up at
5AM to read a dusty book written by some guy who died 50 years ago instead of that
Tim Ferris tome taunting you on your night table, stalking Facebook groups and
interrogating your online friends with FBI-level intensity, trying to uproot someone
competent enough to do it for you, and spinning your wheels as that mega-funnel that
was supposed to launch you into industry-wide stardom gets relegated to back burner
aka never gonna happen status on your Trello dashboard. That's a mouthful."
So one thing you've noticed is sometimes we do like to write these crazy long run-on
sentences. If it's very obvious that you're doing it and just kind of like having one of
those anxious word dumps, that's when you can get away with those long exasperating
sentences. It communicates on a subtle tone that feverish anxiety of doing a million
things. So that's just a little side note on the craft of writing copy and why sometimes
you'll see these long sentences and think, "Yeah, that shouldn't be happening. That's not
very readable." It's not meant to necessarily be super easy to read as it is to
communicate urgency and the anxiety, the intensity of those flames. It's a very anxiety
provoking situation to be in. So that's what we're doing there and we soften it. We give
a few shorter sentences to just kind of let our reader catch up and have that mental
breather.
So "Whew, that's a mouthful. And on our end nothing's launched either. The execution
strategies you'll learn have been hard at work generating millions of dollars, said in an
evil voice, for private clients in the online education and coaching spaces." You'll want to
include a testimonial or two if you don't have that sales page or that sales letter this is
where you will want to bring that social proof vividly into your e-mail.
"The themeplates you'll plug into you funnel have been battle tested and carefully
refined to act as finely tuned switches that kill your iPhone 7 battery from a ceaseless
stream of SamCart or PayPal notifications." So specificity around outcomes also amplify
desire. Then we have the "22 high-converting themeplates and all these materials have
been through alpha, beta, theta, and omega testing with other copywriters, online
entrepreneurs, and course creators to make sure the 'get your ROI in a day' promise
isn't just hot air but available to anyone with an awesome product to sell and a
Pomodoro timer. So with all that said, here's how we're going to get your highest-

So that's kind of evocative and I'm not saying that you would actually do that but it is
kind of this fun empowering feeling and that's what we're trying to go for here. So,
"What's inside 10X launches", so this is gonna be your product walkthrough, leveraging
those Aladdin moments wherever you can. An alternative to going literally like module
by module as your features you can simplify it and make it shorter. Here we did three
core modules, five launch modules, 22 launch e-mail themeplates. So you could break it
down by, not necessarily lesson by lesson, but kind of those high level features. So just
kind of compressing things.
So, "Three core modules that reveal in full detail the Coaching the Conversion method
and how to leverage a new more empowering definition of direct response that opens
up the floodgates to greater intimacy, empathy, and conversions with your audience."
You could give the value to that. "Five launch modules breaking down every single phase
of your launch in full detail, including some you didn't even know existed. You'll learn
exactly what's at play during all those phases and how to quickly craft the messaging
that coaches your prospect into the final conversion in a powerful non-sleazy way."
Then, "None of that BS plug and play, swipe and deploy stuff that shoehorns your
message into lifeless boxes that rob it of all life and power. Instead you'll get full
walkthroughs and examples from the go-to themeplates I turn to over and over again on
six and seven figure launch campaigns so that you can craft your own high-converting e-
mails in about 20 minutes flat and fire them into ConvertKit or whatever you're using
while your coffee's still hot." So this could be the So What Factor. That's the benefit.
Benefits usually follow the words "so what". This is so what it looks like, really kind of
bringing it into that vivid evocative way that they could ground into the context of their
reality, bring those benefits closer and more familiar to where they're at.
Yep. "Hitting send on just one of these can 2X your investment on the spot. Test it."
Then some bonuses that we might be working on. So, "The Launch in Seven bonus
bundle including the Sales Page in a Day Method, The 80/20 Optimizer, and The
Minimum Viable Funnel to make sure that you have no excuses left for not having a
campaign out the door in 7 to 10 days flat." Then you have your real world value. You
have your urgency if it exists. Be honest about it. Your investment. So this is that link.
This is the payment plan.
"But because you've been brave enough to be on this journey with us, yeah I know we
got pretty aggressive with those sacred cows, I want to offer you a little challenge." I like
ending on the challenge. That's what really kind of helps you with that quick valuable
win or that fastest most valuable win. "Take about 30 minutes tomorrow or any of the
59 days after tomorrow to write up one of our themeplates you'll find waiting for you in
the membership area. Hit send. If you feel like you didn't get an instant ROI paired with
a glowing sense of," and this is another one of those kind of like vivid, vivid responses,
what that looks like. "'Oh my God I can do this again tomorrow and the next day for

every campaign or funnel and generate enough automated revenue to hire a team,
build more products, and spend more time on sandy beaches in my zone of genius.'
Then kindly shoot us an e-mail with the subject line 'WTF' and we will refund every
dollar so that you'll only need an extra $18,000 to hire a top copywriter to do the same
work for you."
So kind of cheeky way to position against the alternative, which is having to shell out the
big bucks for an amazing copywriter, which there's nothing wrong with but not
everyone will be in a position to do that. So positioning against that alternative. So,
"Cool? Click here to take the 60 day 10X My ROI Challenge." So this, once again, is a
more speculative e-mail. So things might not be exactly as you saw them in the launch.
But essentially this is what you're going to be doing if you don't have a sales page and
you need to really pack in some of those extra elements, some of the offer stack, the
value, the walkthrough of what they're getting, those features, the benefits they
represent, and the Aladdin moments that really ground it in their reality. Then you're
also gonna be using the quick and valuable win as well as the minimum viable
commitment to close with.
So that is the Aladdin themeplate. Once again, to be used if you're launching something
quickly, you don't have a sales page and you just need your e-mail to do a lot of heavy
lifting. So there's a lot of heavy lifting going on here. If you are gonna roll with it
definitely go through modules six and seven as well so you are steeped in some of those
elements that we're borrowing from those in this e-mail. That is it for launch day. We
did it. Awesome job, guys. I'm gonna catch you ... first I'm gonna catch a breath and
then I'm gonna catch you in the next module, module six, where we're gonna get into
those sales followups, what you're gonna be sending immediately after your cart open
all the way up until 48 hours before your close. So you take a breather, I'm gonna take a
breather, and when you are ready I will catch you in module six.
